channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Everything Announced at Summer Game Fest 2026
item.content:encoded:
Sony took center stage earlier this week with the PlayStation State of Play for 2026, but the multi-platform headliner is here now with Geoff Keighley's Summer Game Fest. Set for many a big game and small game to appear as well as many other oddities that are sure to come up (how will the muppets appear this time?) it's time to see what gaming companies have in store for the next year.

Note this is a live announcement recap post. Announcements are being added as they are made, and if you don't see one here yet please refresh the page and it will be there shortly. Additional details may be added as well as trailers. New announcements will be added to the bottom of the post.

Geoff Keighley took to the stage to kick off the Summer Game Fest, celebrating that this was the 7th year of the Summer Game Fest, and that over the next two hours we'd see plenty games over the next two hours. He continued with some Steam stats, like the fact that nearly 10k games released in the first five months of the year, and that there have been a number of 1m+ sellers on Steam, including by small independent developers.

Resident Evil: Veronica Remake revealed

The first game is set in Paris, and has a horror type feel as we enter a weird building, and find the protagonist's brother has made a mess in his Parisian apartment much to his land lady (or property manager?)'s dismay. One attack later, and we begin to see weird things and a montage of various locations and shots as it is revealed to be the long rumored Resident Evil: Veronica remake and a character is revealed as Claire Redfield. Resident Evil: Veronica is releasing in 2027.

Cuphead Sequel in the Works And More

Studio MDHR took the stage next and announced that they were working on a new Cuphead game that will feature hand animation work there. Additionally, there is another small project in the works at the studio, featured on the era that got them into video game. Mighty Cuphead Adventure is a an 8-bit run and gun platformer.

Alien Isolation 2 Announced

At long last, Sega has announced the sequel to the horror classic Alien Isolation, and we will have Alien Isolation 2 coming in the future. This time, the Alien will be roaming an unsuspecting colony world, taking the horror outside of the limits of a space ship.

GenAtlas from Shadow of the Colossus Creator Fumito Ueda Announced

After being teased previously as Project Robot, Fumito Ueda presented his first title in over a decade since The Last Guardian. GenAtlas is being published by Epic Games, and we don't know much more about it other than that it promises to be quite pretty and likely emotionally devastating.

Blood Message Takes the bloody stage

Samurai and others fight in a desperate battlefield as hordes take the field in Blood Message from 24 entertainment. Ride horses, battle hordes, and explore Japan in this intriguing game.

Stranger Than Heaven Release Date Announced

Masayoshi Yokohama of RGG Studio took to the stage next to talk about Stranger Than Heaven, one of their many Japanese-esq life/yakuza games. Featuring a start studded cast including the likes of Snoop Dog in the trailer and Tupac is also in the game. The Stranger Than Heaven release date was announced, and you'll be able to play the game on January 15th, 2027. 

Snoop Dog then joined Yokohama, Geoff, and translator on stage to talk about how he ended up in the game and how the Tupac estate brought him into the game and a lot of others. Snoop Dog also teased we'll hear more about his Death Row Games in the next few months.

Haex is a Strange New Game from a Strange New Studio

From Dead Astronaut, a studio made up of folks who previously worked on titles like The Division and Little Nightmares in Sweden, Hex is a heavily procedural game that you explore and shoot enemies, while solving weird puzzles and uncovering buried things and watch the landscape around you transform from what you did.

Dead by Daylight celebrates its birthday with more horror

Keeping things vague for now, Dead by Daylight issued an invitation to its 10th birthday bash on June 14th.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/everything-announced-at-summer-game-fest-2026
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Wizards of the Coast Addresses Some Elements of Controversial D&D Beyond Drops
item.content:encoded:
Wizards of the Coast is making changes to how content will be released on D&D Beyond. In response to player feedback and critiques, the company will be revising how D&D Beyond drops will be handled for users on the platform, providing more options for looking at the material.

The Changes to D&D Beyond Drops

In an official post on D&D Beyond, Wizards of the Coast addressed the concerns voiced by the community regarding D&D Beyond drops. For those not aware, the platform started releasing small content drops in May for users who had a Hero or Master tier subscription. These drops were small, providing things like monster statblocks or new spells.

On paper, this isn't an inherently bad idea for ongoing TTRPG support in the digital age. The problem is the way the program was implemented lead to two key issues identified in the post. First, was the inability to content share. If you had a Master tier subscription on D&D Beyond, any books and supplementary material you had bought on the platform could be shared to players in your games. Everything...except the material in D&D content drops.

This is considered an oversight by Dungeons & Dragons players given the conventional wisdom behind TTRPG resources. Generally, new books and supplements are bought by the GM then shared among players. This is true either in physical tables or on VTT platforms. The fact that all content drops were paywalled in this way just adds several unnecessary steps to the play experience.

Wizards of the Coast doesn't have an exact solution to content sharing at this time, but said an update in this matter will be available in the upcoming weeks.

The second issue addressed with D&D Beyond drops is how exactly will this material become available to non-subscribers. On this front, WOTC has confirmed that every May will see a D&D Beyond drop annual bundle, giving all users the chance to buy an entire year's worth of material in one resource on the marketplace.

Generally, this is not a bad solution. R. Talsorian Games, the studio behind Cyberpunk Red, has a similar model with ongoing support with their dark sci-fi TTRPG. Free material gets released as DLC digitally on their websit before getting physically published in softcover Interface books, allowing people who want physical materials to have them. At the time of writing, it is unclear if the content drop bundles will get a similar physical release.

At the time of writing, the second month of D&D Beyond drops has arrived. The drop includes several Corrupted Angel statblocks, five horror-themed battlemaps, three new spells, Searing Orb, Tortoise Shell, and Void Star, and three unique magic items: Amulet of Retributive Healing, Eternal Chalk, and Salubrious Armor.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/wizards-of-coast-addresses-some-elements-of-controversial-dd-beyond-drops
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
